Description: Unique identifier assigned to each participant.
Sequential index indicating the session number within each participant. The first session contained 2000 trials and the second either 1,372 or 1,374.
Unique identifier for a single trial within a session or condition.
Time taken by the participant to respond, in milliseconds.

Unique identifier assigned to each stimulus. Note: There are two versions of each stimulus: a word and a nonword. Whether a certain stimulus is a word or a nonword is indicated by the variable 'lexicality'.
Binary indicator of whether a stimulus is a word or a nonword.
Binary indicator of whether the participant's response was correct.
The linguistic or visual stimulus presented to the participant in a given trial.
University from whose research participant pool the participant was recruited.
Participant's day of birth (YYYY-MM-DD).
Age of the participant at the time of the experiment.
Self-reported or observed gender identity of the participant.
Participant's years of education.
Corrected variable years_of_education. The value was supposed to be years of education but some are recorded as years of university, so values smaller 12 were replaced with value plus 12.
Participant's native or first-acquired language.
Participant's score on the Morningness-Eveningness Questionaire, a circadian rythm questionnare (Horne & Ostberg, 1976).

Number of correct answers given by the participant on the vocabulary test of the Shipley Institute of Living Scale, a self-administering scale for measuring intellectual impairment and deterioration (Shipley, 1940).
Participant's raw score on the vocabulary test of the Shipley Institute of Living Scale, a self-administering scale for measuring intellectual impairment and deterioration (Shipley, 1940).

Vocabulary age of the participant according to the vocabulary test of the Shipley Institute of Living Scale, a self-administering scale for measuring intellectual impairment and deterioration (Shipley, 1940).
Participant's present health score on a general health questionnaire.
Participant's past health score on a general health questionnaire.
Participant's vision score on a general health questionnaire.
Participant's hearing score on a general health questionnaire.
Timestamp when the participant started the final block of the survey. The block contained gender, the MEQ, the Shipley vocabulary test, and the general health questionnaire (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
Timestamp when the participant started the survey session (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
